req.title: External ACH transfer (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall transfer funds to an external ACH-linked account up to $10,000 per transfer; transfers above this shall require step-up verification. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# ACH Transfer Limit & Step-Up Verification — pytest Test Suite

```python
"""
tests/test_ach_transfer_limits.py

Requirement:
    A customer shall transfer funds to an external ACH-linked account up to
    $10,000 per transfer.  Transfers above this limit shall require step-up
    verification.  Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

Design assumptions (adjust imports to match your real application):
    - TransferService.initiate_transfer(account_id, amount, step_up_token=None)
      returns a TransferResult dataclass.
    - TransferResult fields:
        .success        bool
        .requires_step_up  bool
        .error_code     str | None   e.g. "STEP_UP_REQUIRED", "INVALID_AMOUNT"
        .transfer_id    str | None
    - StepUpVerificationService.generate_token(account_id) -> str
    - The service raises ValueError for structurally invalid inputs
      (negative amounts, non-numeric values, etc.).
    - TRANSFER_LIMIT constant = 10_000.00  (USD, two-decimal precision)
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from decimal import Decimal
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import pytest

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Application imports – replace with real paths
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from app.services.transfer_service import TransferService
from app.services.step_up_service import StepUpVerificationService
from app.constants import TRANSFER_LIMIT  # == Decimal("10000.00")
from app.models import TransferResult

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants used throughout the suite
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
LIMIT = Decimal("10_000.00")
ACCOUNT_ID = "ACC-TEST-001"
VALID_STEP_UP_TOKEN = "VALID-STEP-UP-TOKEN-XYZ"
INVALID_STEP_UP_TOKEN = "INVALID-TOKEN"
PENNY = Decimal("0.01")


# ===========================================================================
# Fixtures
# ===========================================================================


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_step_up_service():
    """Provides a mock StepUpVerificationService."""
    svc = MagicMock(spec=StepUpVerificationService)
    svc.generate_token.return_value = VALID_STEP_UP_TOKEN
    svc.verify_token.return_value = True
    return svc


@pytest.fixture()
def transfer_service(mock_step_up_service):
    """
    Real TransferService wired with a mock step-up dependency.
    Adjust constructor signature to match your DI approach.
    """
    return TransferService(step_up_service=mock_step_up_service)


@pytest.fixture()
def valid_step_up_token(mock_step_up_service):
    """Generates a valid step-up token for the test account."""
    return mock_step_up_service.generate_token(ACCOUNT_ID)


# ===========================================================================
# Helper
# ===========================================================================


def _transfer(service, amount, token=None) -> TransferResult:
    """Thin wrapper so tests stay readable."""
    return service.initiate_transfer(
        account_id=ACCOUNT_ID,
        amount=Decimal(str(amount)),
        step_up_token=token,
    )


# ===========================================================================
# 1. BOUNDARY TESTS — exact threshold behaviour
# ===========================================================================


class TestExactBoundaries:
    """
    Critical boundary tests at the defined $10,000 limit.
    Both sides of the boundary must behave correctly (equivalence partitioning
    + boundary value analysis).
    """

    def test_transfer_at_exact_limit_succeeds_without_step_up(self, transfer_service):
        """$10,000.00 is at the limit — must succeed without step-up."""
        result = _transfer(transfer_service, LIMIT)

        assert result.success is True
        assert result.requires_step_up is False
        assert result.error_code is None
        assert result.transfer_id is not None

    def test_transfer_one_penny_above_limit_requires_step_up(self, transfer_service):
        """$10,000.01 is above the limit — must be blocked and flag step-up."""
        amount = LIMIT + PENNY
        result = _transfer(transfer_service, amount)

        assert result.success is False
        assert result.requires_step_up is True
        assert result.error_code == "STEP_UP_REQUIRED"
        assert result.transfer_id is None

    def test_transfer_one_penny_below_limit_succeeds_without_step_up(
        self, transfer_service
    ):
        """$9,999.99 is below the limit — must succeed without step-up."""
        amount = LIMIT - PENNY
        result = _transfer(transfer_service, amount)

        assert result.success is True
        assert result.requires_step_up is False
        assert result.error_code is None
        assert result.transfer_id is not None

    def test_transfer_at_exact_limit_does_not_trigger_step_up_flag(
        self, transfer_service
    ):
        """
        Negative confirmation: $10,000.00 must NOT set requires_step_up
        (i.e., the boundary is inclusive on the allowed side).
        """
        result = _transfer(transfer_service, LIMIT)
        assert result.requires_step_up is False


# ===========================================================================
# 2. WITHIN-LIMIT TRANSFERS (no step-up required)
# ===========================================================================


class TestTransfersWithinLimit:
    """Equivalence class: amounts in (0, 10_000] — all should succeed."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "amount",
        [
            Decimal("0.01"),        # minimum meaningful transfer
            Decimal("1.00"),        # small dollar amount
            Decimal("100.00"),      # typical small transfer
            Decimal("999.99"),      # mid-range
            Decimal("5_000.00"),    # midpoint
            Decimal("9_999.99"),    # one penny below limit
            Decimal("10_000.00"),   # exact limit (boundary)
        ],
    )
    def test_transfer_within_limit_succeeds(self, transfer_service, amount):
        result = _transfer(transfer_service, amount)

        assert result.success is True, (
            f"Expected success for ${amount} but got error_code={result.error_code}"
        )
        assert result.requires_step_up is False
        assert result.transfer_id is not None

    def test_transfer_within_limit_returns_unique_transfer_ids(self, transfer_service):
        """Each successful transfer must produce a unique reference."""
        ids = [_transfer(transfer_service, Decimal("100.00")).transfer_id for _ in range(5)]
        assert len(set(ids)) == 5, "Duplicate transfer IDs detected"


# ===========================================================================
# 3. ABOVE-LIMIT TRANSFERS (step-up required)
# ===========================================================================


class TestTransfersAboveLimit:
    """Equivalence class: amounts > 10_000 — all must require step-up."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "amount",
        [
            Decimal("10_000.01"),   # one penny over (boundary)
            Decimal("10_001.00"),   # one dollar over
            Decimal("15_000.00"),   # 50% over
            Decimal("50_000.00"),   # large transfer
            Decimal("100_000.00"),  # very large transfer
            Decimal("999_999.99"),  # near-maximum practical value
        ],
    )
    def test_transfer_above_limit_without_step_up_is_blocked(
        self, transfer_service, amount
    ):
        result = _transfer(transfer_service, amount)

        assert result.success is False, (
            f"Expected failure for ${amount} but transfer succeeded"
        )
        assert result.requires_step_up is True
        assert result.error_code == "STEP_UP_REQUIRED"
        assert result.transfer_id is None


# ===========================================================================
# 4. STEP-UP VERIFICATION — above-limit transfers with token
# ===========================================================================


class TestStepUpVerification:
    """Transfers above limit should succeed when a valid step-up token is supplied."""

    def test_above_limit_with_valid_step_up_token_succeeds(
        self, transfer_service, valid_step_up_token
    ):
        """$10,000.01 with a valid step-up token must succeed."""
        amount = LIMIT + PENNY
        result = _transfer(transfer_service, amount, token=valid_step_up_token)

        assert result.success is True
        assert result.requires_step_up is False
        assert result.error_code is None
        assert result.transfer_id is not None

    def test_above_limit_with_invalid_step_up_token_is_rejected(
        self, transfer_service
    ):
        """Invalid step-up token must not elevate privileges."""
        amount = LIMIT + PENNY
        result = _transfer(transfer_service, amount, token=INVALID_STEP_UP_TOKEN)

        assert result.success is False
        assert result.error_code in ("INVALID_STEP_UP_TOKEN", "STEP_UP_REQUIRED")
        assert result.transfer_id is None

    def test_above_limit_with_expired_step_up_token_is_rejected(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service
    ):
        """Expired step-up token must be treated the same as no token."""
        mock_step_up_service.verify_token.return_value = False  # token expired
        expired_token = "EXPIRED-TOKEN-ABC"

        amount = LIMIT + Decimal("500.00")
        result = _transfer(transfer_service, amount, token=expired_token)

        assert result.success is False
        assert result.transfer_id is None

    def test_step_up_token_not_required_for_within_limit_transfer(
        self, transfer_service, valid_step_up_token
    ):
        """
        A valid step-up token provided for an under-limit transfer must not
        change outcome — transfer still succeeds (token is ignored).
        """
        result = _transfer(transfer_service, LIMIT, token=valid_step_up_token)
        assert result.success is True

    def test_large_transfer_with_valid_step_up_token_succeeds(
        self, transfer_service, valid_step_up_token
    ):
        """Verify step-up works for significantly large amounts."""
        result = _transfer(transfer_service, Decimal("50_000.00"), token=valid_step_up_token)
        assert result.success is True
        assert result.transfer_id is not None

    def test_step_up_token_can_only_be_used_once(
        self, transfer_service, mock_step_up_service, valid_step_up_token
    ):
        """
        Step-up token must be single-use; second call with same token fails.
        After first use the mock simulates token invalidation.
        """
        amount = LIMIT + PENNY

        # First use — succeeds
        result_1 = _transfer(transfer_service, amount, token=valid_step_up_token)
        assert result_1.success is True

        # Simulate token now invalidated
        mock_step_up_service.verify_token.return_value = False

        # Second use — fails
        result_2 = _transfer(transfer_service, amount, token=valid_step_up_token)
        assert result_2.success is False


# ===========================================================================
# 5. INVALID INPUT VALIDATION
# ===========================================================================


class TestInvalidInputs:
    """Service must reject structurally invalid inputs regardless of limit logic."""

    def test_zero_amount_raises_or_returns_error(self, transfer_service):
        """Zero-value transfers are not meaningful and must be rejected."""
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="amount"):
            _transfer(transfer_service, Decimal("0.00"))

    def test_negative_amount_raises_value_error(self, transfer_service):
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="amount"):
            _transfer(transfer_service, Decimal("-1.00"))

    def test_none_amount_raises_type_error(self, transfer_service):
        with pytest.raises((TypeError, ValueError)):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                account_id=ACCOUNT_ID, amount=None
            )

    def test_string_amount_raises_type_error(self, transfer_service):
        with pytest.raises((TypeError, ValueError)):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                account_id=ACCOUNT_ID, amount="ten thousand"
            )

    def test_missing_account_id_raises_value_error(self, transfer_service):
        with pytest.raises((ValueError, TypeError)):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                account_id=None, amount=LIMIT
            )

    def test_empty_account_id_raises_value_error(self, transfer_service):
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="account_id"):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                account_id="", amount=LIMIT
            )

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_amount", ["10000", 10000, 10000.01])
    def test_non_decimal_numeric_types_raise_type_error(
        self, transfer_service, bad_amount
    ):
        """
        Service must enforce Decimal inputs to avoid floating-point precision
        issues that could incorrectly straddle the boundary.
        """
        with pytest.raises((TypeError, ValueError)):
            transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
                account_id=ACCOUNT_ID, amount=bad_amount
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 6. PRECISION & FLOATING-POINT EDGE CASES
# ===========================================================================


class TestDecimalPrecision:
    """
    Floating-point representation of $10,000.00 can differ from the true
    Decimal value; the service must use exact decimal arithmetic.
    """

    def test_floating_point_representation_does_not_bypass_limit(
        self, transfer_service
    ):
        """
        10000.001 expressed as Decimal must trigger step-up; naive float
        maths might round it to 10000.00 and incorrectly allow it.
        """
        amount = Decimal("10000.001")  # > limit in exact arithmetic
        result = _transfer(transfer_service, amount)

        assert result.requires_step_up is True
        assert result.success is False

    def test_amount_with_many_decimal_places_above_limit_blocked(
        self, transfer_service
    ):
        amount = Decimal("10000.0000000001")
        result = _transfer(transfer_service, amount)
        assert result.success is False
        assert result.requires_step_up is True

    def test_amount_with_many_decimal_places_below_limit_allowed(
        self, transfer_service
    ):
        amount = Decimal("9999.9999999999")
        result = _transfer(transfer_service, amount)
        assert result.success is True
        assert result.requires_step_up is False


# ===========================================================================
# 7. CONCURRENT / IDEMPOTENCY BEHAVIOUR (optional — integration-level)
# ===========================================================================


class TestIdempotencyAndConcurrency:
    """Guard against split-transfer exploits and double-submission."""

    def test_two_concurrent_transfers_each_at_limit_are_independent(
        self, transfer_service
    ):
        """
        Two separate $10,000 transfers must each be evaluated independently;
        there is no per-session accumulation guard at this service layer
        (that belongs to a separate daily-limit service).
        """
        result_1 = _transfer(transfer_service, LIMIT)
        result_2 = _transfer(transfer_service, LIMIT)

        assert result_1.success is True
        assert result_2.success is True
        assert result_1.transfer_id != result_2.transfer_id

    def test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_same_result(self, transfer_service):
        """
        If the caller supplies the same idempotency key twice, the service must
        return the original result without creating a second transfer.
        """
        idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
        result_1 = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
            account_id=ACCOUNT_ID,
            amount=LIMIT,
            idempotency_key=idempotency_key,
        )
        result_2 = transfer_service.initiate_transfer(
            account_id=ACCOUNT_ID,
            amount=LIMIT,
            idempotency_key=idempotency_key,
        )

        assert result_1.transfer_id == result_2.transfer_id


# ===========================================================================
# 8. CONSTANT INTEGRITY
# ===========================================================================


class TestLimitConstant:
    """Sanity-check the application constant so tests remain aligned."""

    def test_transfer_limit_constant_equals_ten_thousand(self):
        assert TRANSFER_LIMIT == Decimal("10000.00"), (
            f"TRANSFER_LIMIT has drifted: expected 10000.00, got {TRANSFER_LIMIT}"
        )

    def test_transfer_limit_constant_is_decimal_type(self):
        assert isinstance(TRANSFER_LIMIT, Decimal), (
            "TRANSFER_LIMIT must be Decimal to avoid floating-point errors"
        )
```

---

## Key Design Decisions

| Concern | Approach |
|---|---|
| **Boundary coverage** | Tests at `$10,000.00`, `$9,999.99`, and `$10,000.01` (BVA) |
| **Equivalence partitioning** | Parametrized suites for *within-limit* and *above-limit* classes |
| **Step-up paths** | Valid token ✓, invalid token ✗, expired token ✗, single-use enforcement |
| **Decimal precision** | Rejects `float`/`str`; tests sub-penny values above the limit |
| **Negative testing** | Zero, negative, `None`, wrong types all raise early |
| **Idempotency** | Duplicate key returns same transfer; concurrent transfers are independent |
| **Constant integrity** | Explicit test prevents silent config drift breaking the limit |
| **Isolation** | `mock_step_up_service` prevents step-up service from polluting unit tests |

      returns a TransferResult dataclass.
    - TransferResult fields:
        .success        bool
        .requires_step_up  bool
        .error_code     str | None   e.g. "STEP_UP_REQUIRED", "INVALID_AMOUNT"
        .transfer_id    str | None

    - TRANSFER_LIMIT constant = 10_000.00  (USD, two-decimal precision)
"""

import uuid

ACCOUNT_ID = "ACC-TEST-001"
VALID_STEP_UP_TOKEN = "VALID-STEP-UP-TOKEN-XYZ"
INVALID_STEP_UP_TOKEN = "INVALID-TOKEN"
PENNY = Decimal("0.01")

# Fixtures
# ===========================================================================


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_step_up_service():

    svc = MagicMock(spec=StepUpVerificationService)
    svc.generate_token.return_value = VALID_STEP_UP_TOKEN
    svc.verify_token.return_value = True
    return svc


@pytest.fixture()
def transfer_service(mock_step_up_service):
    """
    Real TransferService wired with a mock step-up dependency.
    Adjust constructor signature to match your DI approach.
    """
    return TransferService(step_up_service=mock_step_up_service)


@pytest.fixture()
def valid_step_up_token(mock_step_up_service):
    """Generates a valid step-up token for the test account."""
    return mock_step_up_service.generate_token(ACCOUNT_ID)


# ===========================================================================
# Helper
# ===========================================================================


def _transfer(service, amount, token=None) -> TransferResult:
    """Thin wrapper so tests stay readable."""
    return service.initiate_transfer(
        account_id=ACCOUNT_ID,
        amount=Decimal(str(amount)),
        step_up_token=token,
    )


# ===========================================================================
# 1. BOUNDARY TESTS — exact threshold behaviour
# ===========================================================================


class TestExactBoundaries:

    Critical boundary tests at the defined $10,000 limit.
    Both sides of the boundary must behave correctly (equivalence partitioning
    + boundary value analysis).
    """

        """$10,000.00 is at the limit — must succeed without step-up."""
        result = _transfer(transfer_service, LIMIT)

        assert result.success is True
        assert result.requires_step_up is False
        assert
